summary: Joining NDTI in March 2024 represented the culmination of more than two decades of learning, problem-solving, and continuous growth in technology, bringing together nearly every skill I've developed — from customer service and enterprise infrastructure to automation, cloud technologies, cybersecurity, and systems administration. As a Senior Systems Administrator supporting a Department of Defense contractor, my role spans Windows and Linux infrastructure, Microsoft 365 GCC High, Azure, Active Directory, virtualization, networking, security, compliance, and automation. Every day is different: designing infrastructure improvements, responding to production issues, strengthening security, or helping the organization meet strict regulatory requirements like CMMC Level 2, NIST 800-171, and ISO 27001. Automation has become one of my biggest focuses — I regularly use PowerShell, Python, Bash, and Ansible to eliminate repetitive administrative tasks, standardize deployments, and improve configuration consistency across Windows and Linux environments, always aiming to leave systems more reliable and easier for the next administrator to understand. Security is just as central: I've helped implement and maintain Microsoft Entra ID, Conditional Access, SAML authentication, multi-factor authentication, and hybrid identity solutions while supporting our migration into Microsoft GCC High, alongside security hardening scripts and vulnerability remediation. I've also led complex infrastructure work — Active Directory replication recovery, retiring legacy domain controllers, modernizing Microsoft 365 and Azure environments, implementing Fortinet SSL VPN with Entra SAML authentication, upgrading Veeam backup and disaster recovery infrastructure, and improving DNS and network reliability. One project I'm particularly proud of: while implementing SAML authentication, I discovered our VPN infrastructure was under a sustained brute-force attack. Rather than blocking addresses one at a time, I wrote a Python tool that analyzed firewall logs, condensed malicious IP addresses into efficient CIDR ranges, and generated optimized block lists — significantly reducing malicious traffic while making ongoing maintenance far easier. I've also been involved in Microsoft Teams Calling evaluations, endpoint management and imaging, hybrid cloud administration, Azure virtual machines, and Microsoft Intune, and I genuinely enjoy documenting processes, mentoring coworkers, and building repeatable solutions that improve the team's effectiveness over time. Working at NDTI has reinforced what every previous role gradually taught me: the best systems administrators don't simply keep servers running — they enable people to do their best work through reliable, secure, and well-designed technology. Looking back across my whole career, I can see a consistent pattern: I started by helping customers understand technology, grew into supporting enterprise users, then enterprise infrastructure, and today I focus on designing, automating, securing, and improving the systems organizations depend on every day — a journey that continues to shape both my work and the vision behind Ninjatronics.
highlights: Joined NDTI in March 2024 as Senior Systems Administrator supporting a Department of Defense contractor — a role that draws on nearly every skill built across two decades: customer service, enterprise infrastructure, automation, cloud, cybersecurity, and systems administration.

Own infrastructure spanning Windows and Linux, Microsoft 365 GCC High, Azure, Active Directory, virtualization, networking, security, and compliance — including helping meet strict regulatory requirements like CMMC Level 2, NIST 800-171, and ISO 27001.
Use PowerShell, Python, Bash, and Ansible daily to eliminate repetitive administrative work, standardize deployments, and improve configuration consistency across Windows and Linux environments.

Implemented and maintain Microsoft Entra ID, Conditional Access, SAML authentication, MFA, and hybrid identity solutions while supporting the organization's migration into Microsoft GCC High, alongside security hardening scripts and vulnerability remediation.

Led complex infrastructure recovery and modernization work: Active Directory replication recovery, retiring legacy domain controllers, modernizing Microsoft 365 and Azure environments, implementing Fortinet SSL VPN with Entra SAML authentication, upgrading Veeam backup/DR infrastructure, and improving DNS and network reliability.

Discovered a sustained brute-force attack against the VPN infrastructure while implementing SAML authentication, and wrote a Python tool that parsed firewall logs, condensed malicious IPs into efficient CIDR ranges, and generated optimized block lists — one of the projects I'm proudest of.

Extended into Microsoft Teams Calling evaluations, endpoint management and imaging, hybrid cloud administration, Azure virtual machines, and Microsoft Intune as part of ongoing infrastructure modernization.

See a consistent arc across my whole career here: from helping customers understand technology, to enterprise users, to enterprise infrastructure, to now designing, automating, securing, and improving the systems organizations depend on — a journey that continues to shape both my work and the vision behind Ninjatronics.
